observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to:
a) Direct the local health board to furnish the Office of the Auditor the lists of frontline HRHs, and public and private health workers for the grant of COVID-19 AHDP and SRA as determined by them in accordance with Item 4.1 of DBM-DOH Joint Circular Nos 1 & 2, s. 2020 both dated November 25, 2020, for further evaluation of the transactions.
b) Instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to strictly approve transactions in accordance with the provisions of applicable laws, rules and regulations to avoid possible suspension/disallowance in the audit.

We recommended and Management agreed to see to it that policies adopted concerning donations shall adhere with existing applicable accounting and reporting guidelines such as those provided for in Item II of COA Circular No. 2020-009 dated April 21, 2020 to ensure that all donations are properly accounted for.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Acting Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere to the provisions of Items 6.1.5 and 6.1.8.4 of COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015 on the disbursement and corresponding submission of the cash advance voucher, together with the pertinent supporting documents, of the Confidential Funds and/or Intelligence Funds to ensure observance of compliance to the said circular.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant that Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) shall be used for all transactions of the government, whether cash receipts, cash/check disbursements or non-cash transactions pursuant to Section 31 of the Manual of NGAS for LGUs and the latter document shall be the basis for recording the transactions in the appropriate journals.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Treasurer to issue Official Receipts for Intra fund transfers through the issuance of checks to acknowledge receipt of funds by the recipient fund pursuant to Item B, Section 57 of LTOM.

We recommended and Management agreed to enroll the Municipality in the internet banking facility developed by government depository banks in order to generate the monthly bank statements for timely BRS preparation. On the other hand, direct the Acting Municipal Accountant to train the assigned personnel in the preparation of the BRS with close supervision until she acquires the required knowledge or expertise on the aforementioned task.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Allocate funds for the next phase to fast track the completion of the building to be operational/usable according to purpose.
In case of construction by phases, ensure that there is a clear delineation of work for each phase up to its completion, which must be usable, and structurally sound pursuant to Item 4, Annex A of RIRR of RA 9184.

We recommended and Management agreed that the special disbursing officers be designated with the Municipal Mayor's explicit authority, as Head of the Agency, and be bonded to ensure indemnity in case of theft or loss.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended management to strictly comply with the minimum standards and operating requirements of a controlled dump in accordance to Sections 39 and 41 of RA 9003. 

Further, we recommended the following to address the challenges in the existing sanitary landfill:

a.	Maintain the drainage system to ensure proper flow of water;
b.	Cover procedure befitting the existing condition using landfill equipment to promote lateral runoff of precipitation and to prevent ponding. Grades shall be established of sufficient slopes to account for future settlement of the fill surface; and

c.	Maintenance of access road to facilitate better transport of collected solid waste.
observations.managementAction: Directed the ESWM Officer to strictly follow Section 22 of RA 9003 on the proper segregation and storage of solid waste pending for collection.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to ensure that proper segregation and storage of solid waste pending for collection is in accordance to Section 22 of RA 9003.

observations.reason: Validation revealed that   the lack of plan of segregation and manpower in the ESWM Office hindered in the full implementation of the recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended management to strictly comply with the minimum standards and operating requirements of a controlled dump in accordance to Sections 39 and 41 of RA 9003. 

Further, we recommended the following to address the challenges in the existing sanitary landfill:

a.	Maintain the drainage system to ensure proper flow of water;
b.	Cover procedure befitting the existing condition using landfill equipment to promote lateral runoff of precipitation and to prevent ponding. Grades shall be established of sufficient slopes to account for future settlement of the fill surface; and
c.	Maintenance of access road to facilitate better transport of collected solid waste.
observations.managementAction: Directed the ESWM Officer to strictly follow Section 22 of RA 9003 on the proper segregation and storage of solid waste pending for collection.
observations.reason: lack of plan of segregation and manpower in the ESWM Office hindered in the full implementation of the recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Local Chief Executive to ensure the maximum utilization of the 20% development fund so that infrastructure projects are efficiently and effectively implemented during the year to provide socio-economic development.

We also recommended the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to thoroughly plan the annual procurement activities and stop the practice of conducting public bidding for infrastructure projects in the last quarter of the year to preclude the delayed implementation of the projects. Moreover, the municipal government should strengthen its monitoring of the projects so that appropriate information on the status of the projects will be given to the public and other stakeholders.
observations.managementAction: Management issued memorandum to the Municipal Engineering Office, Municipal Planning and Development Office, and the Municipal Development Council to thresh out the problems on the implementation of projects and formulate measures to prevent these problems from recurring in 2017.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended management to:
Assess the impact of the significant delays in the procurement process of the municipality in coordination with the concerned offices and introduce improvements in the soonest possible time to achieve efficient delivery of goods and services.
Instruct the MTO/BAC to revisit the procurement rules and documentary requirements taking into consideration the timelines set by RA 9184; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to closely coordinate with the MTO on the basic and additional documents for all transactions to facilitate the processing of the DVs.
observations.managementAction: Management called the attention of the concerned offices and personnel especially the BAC to review procurement rules and procedure to avoid delays in the procurement process.
observations.reason: Non-coordination among the MTO, BA, Municipal Accountant and the department heads barred the m to streamline the procurement activities and observed the basic and additional documentary requirements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to indicate in all purchase orders complete information, such as the mode of procurement, printed name of person signing the purchase order, date of receipt terms and condition, date of delivery, etc., as required in COA Circular No. 96-010.
observations.managementAction: All concerned offices have been directed to comply the recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the concerned LGU officials and employees adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 by furnishing the Office of the Auditor with copies of contracts and purchase orders and its supporting documents within five (5) days after perfection.
observations.managementAction: Management instructed the BAC secretariat to submit all the contracts and purchase orders to COA five days after its perfection.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management direct the Municipal Accountant to assign an accounting staff to religiously prepare the Report on the Sources and Utilization of DRRMF on a monthly basis. Likewise, require the MDRRM Officer to submit such report on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the MDRRMC and the Local Development Council to the COA Auditor as required in COA Circular 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: Management instructed the Municipal Accountant to comply the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management instruct the Local School Board to prioritize for funding the programs, projects and activities stipulated in Section 272 of the Local Government Code and DECS-DBM-DILG Joint Circular Nos. 01 s. 1998 dated April 14, 1998, 01-A dated March 14, 2000 and 01-B dated June 25, 2001 in the Annual and Supplemental Budget for SEF.
observations.managementAction: Called the attention of Local Board and District Supervisors to observe compliance of the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that LCE establish the municipality’s Inventory Committee and immediately conduct the physical inventory of all inventory and property accounts; instruct the MTO to take charge the receipt, storage and issuance of inventories, and prepare the appropriate SSMI upon issuance; and the MAO based its records to appropriate source documents before recording expenses for these items. The MAO to verify the balances of these inventories based on the physical inventory report and draw adjusting entries, if necessary.

We recommend that Municipal Treasurer prepare the certified list of real property tax receivable regularly as the basis for the setting up of the RPT and SET Receivables by the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year. Likewise, the Municipal Accountant to analyze and reconcile the differences noted to these receivables account. On the other hand, the Management considers the technological and workforce requirements of MTO to efficiently report, compute and summarize the list of RPT receivables for the given year.

We recommend that Management to fast-track the registration of lands under the name of the Municipality of Tagbina so that its ownership will be conclusive, guaranteed to be indefeasible, unassailable, and imprescriptible. Further, instruct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare RPCPPE for the Land account.

We recommend that Municipal Accounting Office and the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to continue the observance of the internal control put in place to continually enhance the presentation of its current and long-term obligations in the Financial Statements.

We recommend Management to instruct the personnel in-charge for the preparation of payroll to continue observing the order of preference authorized deductions should be applied against the employees’ salaries, but also comply with the mandatory monthly net take home pay pursuant to RA No. 10964.

We recommend the Management to instruct the Municipal Accounting Office to thoroughly examine the BIR registration documents of the job orders, contract of services and casual personnel, and to be updated and knowledgeable on new rules and regulations on the collection, withholding and remittance of tax in order to collect and withhold the correct amount of tax.

We recommend Municipal Treasurer to prepare the detailed list of delinquent real properties showing the history of delinquency of each RPUs to institute appropriate administrative action, particularly to those delinquent for a long period of time, for the collection of delinquent RPT.

We recommend that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to continue the withholding of refund from the payroll and other claims of the liable personnel from audit disallowances which became final and executory. Moreover, execute the appropriate actions to claim the disallowances for personnel who are no longer connected to the LGU.

We recommend Management that intensive information and education campaign on ESWM be conducted, close coordination with the MESWM and BESWM Boards on institutionalizing effective policies on solid waste management, provision of necessary appropriation for the operation of controlled dump, and consider the provision of incentives pursuant to Section 45 Chapter IV of RA No. 9003. In addition, fast track the completion of Terms of Reference on the construction of sanitary landfill.

We recommend that GAD Focal Point System continue the observance of the provisions of the JMC to effectively addressed gender issues and concern of the municipality. In addition, immediately submit the GAD Accomplishment Report for CY 2018 for verification.

We recommend that concerned offices fast-track the preparation of the POW, the project monitoring plan and the project procurement monitoring plan for the implementation of these PPAs, and the procurement process. In addition, instruct barangays to determine its priority PPAs to avoid delay and/or non-implementation.
We recommend that LDRRMO prepare the project procurement management plan and/or activity/training design and POW, and observe the timeline for implementation provided on the LDRRMF Investment Plan.

We recommend that Municipal Engineer prepare the project monitoring plan and/or strictly observed the timetable of implementation for these PPAs. The Management to impose liquidated damages, if necessary, for delayed completion of PPAs by the contractor.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended management to strictly comply with the minimum standards and operating requirements of a controlled dump in accordance to Sections 39 and 41 of RA 9003. 

Further, we recommended the following to address the challenges in the existing sanitary landfill:

a.	Maintain the drainage system to ensure proper flow of water;
b.	Cover procedure befitting the existing condition using landfill equipment to promote lateral runoff of precipitation and to prevent ponding. Grades shall be established of sufficient slopes to account for future settlement of the fill surface; and
c.	Maintenance of access road to facilitate better transport of collected solid waste.

observations.reason: lack of plan of segregation and manpower in the ESWM Office hindered in the full implementation of the recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Local Chief Executive to ensure the maximum utilization of the 20% development fund so that infrastructure projects are efficiently and effectively implemented during the year to provide socio-economic development.

We also recommended the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to thoroughly plan the annual procurement activities and stop the practice of conducting public bidding for infrastructure projects in the last quarter of the year to preclude the delayed implementation of the projects. Moreover, the municipal government should strengthen its monitoring of the projects so that appropriate information on the status of the projects will be given to the public and other stakeholders.
observations.managementAction: Management issued memorandum to the Municipal Engineering Office, Municipal Planning and Development Office, and the Municipal Development Council to thresh out the problems on the implementation of projects and formulate measures to prevent these problems from recurring in 2017.
observations.reason: Some of the infra projects were not implemented due to the late conduct of public bidding; some are for preparation of POWs, detailed estimates and designs caused by lack of manpower in the Municipal Engineering Office. Moreover, the delayed enactment of the 2017 executive budget also caused the untimely implementation of the infra projects.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended management to:
Assess the impact of the significant delays in the procurement process of the municipality in coordination with the concerned offices and introduce improvements in the soonest possible time to achieve efficient delivery of goods and services.
Instruct the MTO/BAC to revisit the procurement rules and documentary requirements taking into consideration the timelines set by RA 9184; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to closely coordinate with the MTO on the basic and additional documents for all transactions to facilitate the processing of the DVs.
observations.managementAction: Management called the attention of the concerned offices and personnel especially the BAC to review procurement rules and procedure to avoid delays in the procurement process.
observations.reason: Non-coordination among the MTO, BA, Municipal Accountant and the department heads barred the m to streamline the procurement activities and observed the basic and additional documentary requirements.
